features.claim: Antigravity CLI scopes conversation histories to the current working directory, preventing context pollution across projects.
features.name: Workspace scoping
features.quote: Antigravity CLI scopes conversation histories directly to your current working directory. When you launch `agy` from a specific directory, the agent only displays and resume sessions associated with that specific local repository or subdirectory.

features.claim: Users can return to prior conversations to continue an implementation or refine an existing solution.
features.name: Resuming sessions
features.quote: You can return to a prior conversation to continue an implementation or refine an existing solution.

features.claim: An interactive Conversation Picker overlay lets users search, filter, and resume prior sessions within the terminal UI.
features.name: Resuming via the TUI session picker
features.quote: Type `/resume` in the prompt box and press `Enter`. The interactive Conversation Picker overlay opens. Start typing keyword terms to filter conversations by description or ID.

features.claim: CLI users can import and resume conversation threads originally started in the Antigravity 2.0 desktop visual editor.
features.name: Importing conversations from Antigravity 2.0
features.quote: If you are utilizing the public version of Antigravity CLI, you can import and resume active threads initiated within the Antigravity 2.0 desktop visual editor.

features.claim: Launching with --continue instantly resumes the most recent session, or a specific session can be loaded by passing its UUID.
features.name: Quick resume via the command line
features.quote: To instantly resume the single most recent session in your active workspace without entering the picker, launch the executable with the `--continue` flag.

features.claim: The /fork command clones the entire conversation history into a new independent session, enabling safe parallel experimentation with alternate approaches.
features.name: Branching with `/fork`
features.quote: The `/fork` command clones your entire conversation history up to the current turn into a new, independent session.
